description: A thin glass cup is 'welua' if a small impact could crack it. A young plant can be 'welua' when even ordinary pressure may damage it. Fragility and strength can coexist when the forces or conditions differ.
articulatory_notes: For voiced 'w', round the lips and lift the back of the tongue before unrounding into true-mid front 'e'. Set the tongue tip against the alveolar ridge for 'l', then move into stressed rounded high-back 'u'. Keep final central 'a' separate in hiatus.
usage_notes: 'welua lopha' is a fragile vessel. A thing may be 'kethua' (hard) and still 'welua'; glass is the familiar case.

semantic_domains.physical: easy breakage or damage under comparatively small force
